2605.07306.title: BioProVLA-Agent: An Affordable, Protocol-Driven, Vision-Enhanced VLA-Enabled Embodied Multi-Agent System with Closed-Loop-Capable Reasoning for Biological Laboratory Manipulation
2605.07306.paper_summary: Biological laboratory automation can reduce repetitive manual work and improve reproducibility, but reliable embodied execution in wet-lab environments remains challenging. Protocols are often unstructured, labware is frequently transparent or reflective, and multi-step procedures require state-aware execution beyond one-shot instruction following. Existing robotic systems often rely on costly hardware, fixed workflows, dedicated instruments, or robotics-oriented interfaces. Here, we introduce BioProVLA-Agent, an affordable, protocol-driven, vision-enhanced embodied multi-agent system enabled by Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models for biological manipulation. The system uses protocols as the task interface and integrates protocol parsing, visual state verification, and embodied execution in a closed-loop workflow. A Tailored LLM Protocol Agent converts protocols into verifiable subtasks; a VLM-RAG Verification Agent assesses readiness and completion using observations, robot states, retrieved knowledge, and success/failure examples; and a VLA Embodied Agent executes verified subtasks through a lightweight policy. To improve robustness under wet-lab visual perturbations, we develop AugSmolVLA, an online augmentation strategy targeting transparent labware, reflections, illumination shifts, and overexposure. We evaluate the system on a hierarchical benchmark covering 15 atomic tasks, 6 composite workflows, and 3 bimanual tasks, including tube loading, sorting, waste disposal, cap twisting, and liquid pouring. Across normal and high-exposure settings, AugSmolVLA improves execution stability over ACT, X-VLA, and the original SmolVLA, especially for precise placement, transparent-object manipulation, composite workflows, and visually degraded scenes. These results suggest a practical route toward accessible, protocol-centered, and verification-capable embodied AI for biological manipulation.
2605.07306.paper_summary_zh: 生物實驗室自動化可以減少重複的手動工作並提高可重複性，但在濕實驗室環境中可靠的具身執行仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/> 協議通常是非結構化的，實驗器材經常是透明或反光的，多步驟程序需要超越單次指令執行的狀態感知執行。<lb/> 現有的機器人系統通常依賴昂貴的硬體、固定的工作流程、專用的儀器或以機器人為導向的介面。<lb/> 在此，我們介紹了 BioProVLA-Agent，一種經濟實惠的、以協議驅動的、增強視覺的具身多智能體系統，該系統由視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型支持，用於生物操作。<lb/> 該系統使用協議作為任務介面，並在閉環工作流程中整合協議解析、視覺狀態驗證和具身執行。<lb/> 一個量身定制的 LLM 協議代理將協議轉換為可驗證的子任務；一個 VLM-RAG 驗證代理使用觀察、機器人狀態、檢索的知識和成功/失敗示例來評估準備和完成情況；而一個 VLA 具身代理則通過輕量級策略執行已驗證的子任務。<lb/> 為了在濕實驗室視覺擾動下提高穩健性，我們開發了 AugSmolVLA，一種針對透明實驗器材、反射、照明變化和過度曝光的在線增強策略。<lb/> 我們在一個涵蓋 15 個原子任務、6 個複合工作流程和 3 個雙手任務的分層基準上評估該系統，包括管子裝載、分類、廢物處理、蓋子扭轉和液體倒入。<lb/> 在正常和高曝光設置下，AugSmolVLA 在執行穩定性上優於 ACT、X-VLA 和原始的 SmolVLA，特別是在精確放置、透明物體操作、複合工作流程和視覺退化場景中。<lb/> 這些結果表明，朝著可及的、以協議為中心和具驗證能力的具身 AI 進行生物操作的實用路徑。
2605.07306.author: Zhaohui Du et.al.
2605.07306.authors: Zhaohui Du, Zhe Wang, Hongmei Fei, Xiwen Cao, Ting Xiao, Qi Wang, Huanbo Jin, Jiaming Gu, Quan Lu, Zhe Liu
